question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be between $100,000 and $102,000 on November 12?

Description: This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

evidences.content: 0.14% Bitcoin is trading essentially flat near $64,000, holding above key support after a sharp intraday swing from $63,784 to $65,469. The primary narrative centers on a meaningful deceleration in spot ETF outflows — weekly redemptions fell roughly 87% from early June peaks — signaling the heaviest phase of institutional selling may be passing. Strategy added another 520 BTC for ~$35 million, bringing its total to 847,363 BTC, reinforcing corporate accumulation at these levels. Macro headwinds persist as Fed Chair Warsh's hawkish stance has pushed rate-hike odds for 2026 to ~85%, while DXY near 101 and 10-year yields around 4.5% keep liquidity tight. The CLARITY Act's Senate path remains uncertain, with bipartisan ethics provisions stalling progress ahead of the August recess. [...] Bitcoin is trading essentially flat, hovering near $63,100 after a volatile session that saw an intraday high near $64,200 before sellers pushed back. evidences.title: Bitcoin price volatility ramps up around FOMC days — Will this time ...
evidences.content: Investors could also be anticipating a possible short squeeze. If traders were expecting Bitcoin to drop and positioned short, a sudden increase in ETF inflows could play a role in traders’ behaviors and trigger a short squeeze. Following the FOMC, BTC’s price action, along with onchain data and spot ETF flows will show whether the recent activity was part of a long-term accumulation trend or just speculative positioning. However, one thing that many traders agree on now is that BTC could experience a significant price movement after the FOMC announcement. As crypto trader Master of Crypto put it in a recent X post: Even without rate cuts, the chance of the Fed issuing dovish statements could lift markets, while the absence of them could drive prices lower. [...] On March 17, the spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $275 million in net inflows, marking a shift from a month of outflows. This may signal a shift in investor sentiment and expectations regarding the Fed’s policy decisions. If spot ETF inflows are rising before the FOMC, investors might be anticipating a more dovish stance from the Fed, such as signaling future rate cuts or maintaining liquidity-friendly policies. evidences.content: Keywords: Bitcoin; market microstructure; order flow; queueing theory; market quality; liquidity risk; digital assets; market resiliency Graphical Abstract ## 1. Introduction Binance spot BTC/USDT is a useful venue for studying short-horizon liquidity and resiliency because it combines continuous high-turnover trading with auditable trade prints and aggressor-side information. Those features support venue-level diagnostics built directly from transaction data and make it possible to track how trading activity, signed flow, and recovery conditions evolve through time inside a single market. evidences.title: Cryptocurrency Market Dynamics Around Bitcoin Futures Expiration Events - QuantPedia
evidences.content: However, back on topic! Since we have an absolutely minuscule (weeks) sample of spot ETFs, events, and surroundings, their consequent influences on future prices during expiration have yet to be shown. We will leave any guesses open. What will definitely be interesting is to see if trad-fin “classical” events will emanate on spot ETFs and, therefore, influence spot and futures prices; we are talking about holiday effects, the turn of the months, etc. All of that is yet to be seen. # Main Results and Commentary [...] Primarily used in commodity/currency/rates and equity markets, they have also found use in cryptocurrency markets, utilizing theoretical aspects of perpetual (BTC-PERP) futures (Shiller, Robert J, 1993). It is important to note that PERPs are (or at least at the beginning were) traded on unregulated exchanges (often with leverage up to 100x) but recently were added to respected ones such as Coinbase (which is traded on NASDAQ as ticker COIN) too. Perpetual futures have no expiration dates, but traditional futures do. Therefore, we are interested in the impact of the introduction of traditional highly liquid futures on the CME Exchange and their expiration calendar on...

evidences.title: Binance trading data reveals why Bitcoin prices are sliding even as spot buyers flood the market with bids
evidences.content: This is also why visible order book support is a weaker concept than it looks on a chart. Displayed bids can be real, but they're conditional. They can be pulled, layered, refreshed, or simply outpaced by the volume coming from the larger derivatives complex. Order books are records of resting intent, not execution guarantees. ## What the data shows The Binance BTC/USDT perpetual futures versus spot volume ratio is the cleanest starting point because it quantifies where activity is concentrated. On Feb. 3, the perpetual-to-spot volume ratio read 7.87, with $23.51 billion in perpetual volume against $2.99 billion in spot while BTC traded around $75,770. On Feb. 5, the ratio was still 6.12, with $15.97 billion in perps volume against $2.61 billion in spot, and the price near $69,700. [...] Perpetual and dated futures don't mint coins, but they do create a second market that can become larger, faster, and more reflexive than spot. Perps are designed to track spot through a funding mechanism and can be traded with leverage, which means a relatively small amount of collateral can control a much larger notional position. That combination tends to pull activity into derivatives when tr...

evidences.content: ##### `--data-type (-t)` (spot) klines aggTrades trades ##### `--data-type (-t)` (usd-m/coin-m monthly) aggTrades bookTicker fundingRate indexPriceKlines klines markPriceKlines premiumIndexKlines trades ##### `--data-type (-t)` (coin-m daily) aggTrades bookDepth bookTicker indexPriceKlines klines liquidationSnapshot markPriceKlines metrics premiumIndexKlines trades ##### `--data-type (-t)` (usd-m daily) aggTrades bookDepth bookTicker indexPriceKlines klines markPriceKlines metrics premiumIndexKlines trades ##### `--data-type (-t)` (option) BVOLIndex EOHSummary ##### `--intervals (-i)` 1s 1m 3m 5m 15m 30m 1h 2h 4h 6h 8h 12h 1d 3d 1w 1mo. ### Output directory [...] ### Output directory By default the data is saved in the current directory. Pass `-o` or `--output` followed by a relative or absolute path to change that. Data is loaded with a stream. Until the file is fully downloaded and verified, it will look like this: `...UNVERIFIED.zip`. ### Concurrency By default 5 files are downloaded at a time. Use `-P` to change the number (pass `-P 1` to download each file sequentially). If you get `(no data)` for a file, it's likely that Binance does not have the data for the chosen market...

evidences.title: Why bitcoin institutional demand is on the rise
evidences.content: ### Futures BTC futures contracts traded on the CME were launched in December 2017 and provide an avenue for institutional investors to gain derivative exposure to BTC, allowing investors to speculate on the future price of BTC without directly holding the underlying asset. Some advantages to futures is that they are regulated, can allow for leveraged exposure to potentially amplify gains (but losses will also be amplified), and they can be used to hedge BTC exposure. But keep in mind basis risk and the cost of carry and roll. ## Embracing BTC as an institutional asset [...] Inflation An overall increase in the price of an economy’s goods and services during a given period, translating to a loss in purchasing power per unit of currency. Inflation generally occurs when growth of the money supply outpaces growth of the economy. Central banks attempt to limit inflation, and avoid deflation, in order to keep the economy running smoothly. Proof of work (PoW) A consensus mechanism used by many blockchains, including BTC, where miners compete to solve cryptographic puzzles to validate transactions and create new blocks, earning BTC in the process. BTC follows a PoW structure to validat...

evidences.title: A dive into liquidity demographics for crypto asset trading | S&P Global
evidences.content: Figure 17 shows the market depth on Binance for the asset pair EUR-USDT in euros for the 1% and 10% band (up and down) from the midpoint of the bid-ask price. Of note is the shallow market of less than €5 million for the 1% band and less than €8 million for the cumulative 10% band. This is not surprising as stablecoins have more utility on-chain than off-chain. By contrast, on Binance, USDT liquidity is much greater against other digital assets such as ETH and BTC than against fiat markets.Figure 18 shows a much higher market depth for BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT for the 1% band around the mid-price of USDT 15 million and USDT 30 million, respectively. [...] Figure 19 shows the average 1% market depth for selected markets (Binance) is lowest for EUR-USDC and highest for BTC-USDT. Crypto-USDT markets show a significantly higher market depth than crypto-fiat markets, which involve banks, fees, know your customer and other compliance measures. Crypto-USDT does not have any traditional banking constraints and is integrated seamlessly into trading strategies. External events or market news can affect market depth. During events of high volatility, such as the Silicon Valley Bank crisis in...

evidences.title: High-frequency dynamics of Bitcoin futures - ScienceDirect.com
evidences.content: features across statistical, temporal, and spectral (FFT) domains. The study capture the latent algorithmic heartbeats that dictate price discovery when standard liquidity windows are compressed by over 80%. The resulting high-dimensional data is partitioned using K-Means clustering, which identifies five distinct behavioral archetypes, a solution validated by a Hierarchical Dendrogram. The analysis reveals a significant bifurcation of the market, visually confirmed by the PCA projection. A large Mainstream Herd (Clusters 0 and 1) is distinguished by moderate persistence (Cluster 0) and rapid mean-reversion (Cluster 1). The spectral analysis identifies dominant 15-minute and 8-minute cycles, suggesting the positive returns were driven by systematic, rhythmic bursts of institutional [...] were driven by systematic, rhythmic bursts of institutional buying. The presence of idiosyncratic Information Pockets proves that even in high-sentiment, ceremonial sessions, structural algorithmic constraints and idiosyncratic news can override global market inertia. The findings challenge the notion of a monolithic market response, revealing a fragmented ecosystem governed by Bounded Rationali...
